column1: Clock-target hierarchy in GSE221103 (Altman/Dang 2023 PubMed 37639465) — per-condition
Source: Circadian time-series RNA-seq of inducible N-MYC-ER human neuroblastoma cells
Protocol: RNA collected every 4h for 52h after dexamethasone entrainment. MYC-ON = 4-OHT treatment 24h prior; MYC-OFF = vehicle
N=14 time points per condition. AR(2) eigenvalue modulus (|λ|) on log2-normalised mean-centred expression data
Section A — Group-level summary (clock mean vs target mean)
Section B — Per-gene clock |λ| for all four conditions
- MYC-ON increases clock |λ| in both cell lines; effect is larger in SKNAS (MYCN-amplified)
- MYC-ON leaves target |λ| approximately unchanged (SHEP +0.015; SKNAS -0.010)
- Clock-target gap widens under MYC-ON in both cell lines; clocks are NOT suppressed but locked high
- This is inconsistent with simple BMAL1 suppression; instead consistent with constitutive non-oscillatory clock gene expression
- Per-gene data: — indicates gene absent or below quality threshold in that condition
- N=14 time points; predicted individual-gene AR(2) bias ±0.08 at this resolution
